_instructions.population: One concept = the DOMAIN being studied. List synonyms comma-separated. These are AND-ed with other slots in PubMed.
_instructions.intervention: One concept = the METHOD or TOOL. List synonyms comma-separated (most specific first). First 3 are used for S2 keyword query.
_instructions.outcome: IMPORTANT: Use DOMAIN-SPECIFIC terms only. Generic words like 'precision', 'recall', 'accuracy' appear in thousands of unrelated medical papers and will destroy PubMed precision. Use methodology-specific phrases like 'title screening', 'PRISMA flow', 'data extraction'.
_instructions.domain_keywords: Used by the LLM refiner to block off-topic expansion suggestions. Auto-derived from PICO if left empty.
_instructions.S2_query_note: Semantic Scholar receives a KEYWORD query, not the research question sentence. The system builds this automatically from your PICO slots.

research_question: How do AI agents and large language models automate systematic literature review?
population: systematic review, literature review, scoping review, evidence synthesis
intervention: large language model, LLM, GPT, AI agent, machine learning, NLP, natural language processing
outcome: title screening, abstract screening, PRISMA flow, data extraction, quality assessment
